# targets

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `targets`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `targets.darwin.copyApps.directory` | `string` | Path to link apps relative to the home directory. |
| `targets.darwin.copyApps.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable copying macOS applications to the user environment (works with Spotlight). |
| `targets.darwin.copyApps.enableChecks` | `boolean` | Whether to enable enable App Management checks. |
| `targets.darwin.currentHostDefaults` | `open submodule of attribute set of attribute set of anything` | Set macOS user defaults. Unlike targets.darwin.defaults , the preferences will only be applied to the currently logged-in host. This distinction is important for networked accounts. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults` | `open submodule of attribute set of attribute set of anything` | Set macOS user defaults. Values set to null are ignored. |
| `targets.darwin.keybindings` | `attribute set of anything` | This will configure the default keybindings for text fields in macOS applications. See Apple‚Äôs documentation for more details. |
| `targets.darwin.linkApps.directory` | `string` | Path to link apps relative to the home directory. |
| `targets.darwin.linkApps.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable linking macOS applications to the user environment. |
| `targets.darwin.search` | `null or one of ‚ÄúBing‚Äù, ‚ÄúDuckDuckGo‚Äù, ‚ÄúEcosia‚Äù, ‚ÄúGoogle‚Äù, ‚ÄúYahoo‚Äù` | Default search engine. |
| `targets.genericLinux.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable settings that make Home Manager work better on GNU/Linux distributions other than NixOS. |
| `targets.genericLinux.extraXdgDataDirs` | | |
| `targets.genericLinux.gpu.drivers` | | |
| `targets.genericLinux.gpu.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable GPU driver integration for non-NixOS systems. |
| `targets.genericLinux.gpu.nixStateDirectory` | `absolute path` | The path to the Nix state directory. This only needs to be changed from default if the path was overridden, e.g., by setting the NIX_STATE_DIR environment variable. |
| `targets.genericLinux.gpu.nvidia.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable proprietary Nvidia drivers. |
| `targets.genericLinux.gpu.nvidia.sha256` | `null or string matching the pattern sha256-.*=` | The hash of the downloaded driver file. It can be obtained by running, for example, |
| `targets.genericLinux.gpu.nvidia.version` | `null or string matching the pattern [0-9]{3}\.[0-9]{2,3}(\.[0-9]{2,3})?` | The exact version of Nvidia drivers to use. This version must match the version of the driver used by the host OS. |
| `targets.genericLinux.gpu.packages` | `attribute set` | The Nixpkgs package set where drivers are taken from. |
| `targets.genericLinux.gpu.setupPackage` | `package (read only)` | Resulting setup package. |
| `targets.genericLinux.nixGL.defaultWrapper` | `one of ‚Äúmesa‚Äù, ‚ÄúmesaPrime‚Äù, ‚Äúnvidia‚Äù, ‚ÄúnvidiaPrime‚Äù` | The package wrapper function available for use as (config.lib.nixGL.wrap <package>) . Intended to start programs on the main GPU. |
| `targets.genericLinux.nixGL.installScripts` | `null or (list of (one of ‚Äúmesa‚Äù, ‚ÄúmesaPrime‚Äù, ‚Äúnvidia‚Äù, ‚ÄúnvidiaPrime‚Äù))` | For each wrapper wrp named in the provided list, a wrapper script named nixGLWrp is installed into the environment. These scripts are useful for running programs not installed via Home Manager. |
| `targets.genericLinux.nixGL.offloadWrapper` | `one of ‚Äúmesa‚Äù, ‚ÄúmesaPrime‚Äù, ‚Äúnvidia‚Äù, ‚ÄúnvidiaPrime‚Äù` | The package wrapper function available for use as (config.lib.nixGL.wrapOffload <package>) . Intended to start programs on the secondary GPU. |
| `targets.genericLinux.nixGL.packages` | `null or (attribute set)` | The nixGL package set containing GPU library wrappers. This can be used to provide OpenGL and Vulkan access to applications on non-NixOS systems by using (config.lib.nixGL.wrap <package>) for the default wrapper, or (config.lib.nixGL.wrappers.<wrapper> <package>) for any available wrapper. |
| `targets.genericLinux.nixGL.prime.card` | `string` | Selects the non-default graphics card used for PRIME render offloading. The value can be: |
| `targets.genericLinux.nixGL.prime.installScript` | `null or one of ‚Äúmesa‚Äù, ‚Äúnvidia‚Äù` | If this option is set, the wrapper script prime-offload is installed into the environment. It allows starting programs on the secondary GPU selected by the nixGL.prime.card option. This makes sense when the program is not already using one of nixGL PRIME wrappers, or for programs not installed from Nixpkgs. |
| `targets.genericLinux.nixGL.prime.nvidiaProvider` | `null or string` | If this option is set, it overrides the offload provider for Nvidia PRIME offloading. Consult the proprietary Nvidia driver documentation on the \_\_NV_PRIME_RENDER_OFFLOAD_PROVIDER environment variable. |
| `targets.genericLinux.nixGL.vulkan.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Vulkan in nixGL wrappers. |
